libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment or system properties are required. Keep tests self-contained: create a temporary WEB-INF/web.xml/static resources and start an embedded Jetty Server/WebAppContext on an ephemeral localhost port. If testing WebAppClassLoader.Context on 9.4.0.RC1, implement the new signatures isSystemClass(Class<?>), isServerClass(Class<?>), isServerResource(String, URL), and isSystemResource(String, URL), rather than the older 9.3 String-based methods.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8856 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-webapp:9.4.0.RC1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Optional Jetty features such as JSP, annotations scanning, JMX, JNDI, or WebSocket support may require additional Jetty modules, but they are not needed for core jetty-webapp coverage and should not be introduced unless the generated test intentionally targets those optional features.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is required for meaningful jetty-webapp coverage. The artifact transitively brings the Jetty server/servlet/security/xml stack and javax.servlet-api; normal use can be exercised with an embedded Server and WebAppContext using temporary webapp files. The reported javac failure is due to WebAppClassLoader.Context API changes in 9.4.0.RC1, not missing setup.
